---
title: "2. Linear Programming with PuLP"
---

## Introduction

Linear Programming (LP) is a core technique in Operations Research used to optimize a linear objective subject to linear constraints.

In this chapter we solve the classic **Diet Problem**.

## Mathematical Formulation

**Decision variables** ($x_1$ = servings of Steak, $x_2$ = Chicken, $x_3$ = Rice, $x_4$ = Broccoli)

$$
\begin{align*}
\text{Minimize } & 2.5x_1 + 1.8x_2 + 0.5x_3 + 0.6x_4 \\
\text{s.t.} \quad
& 5x_1 + 6x_2 + x_3 + 2x_4 \geq 50 \quad \text{(Protein)} \\
& 2x_1 + x_2 + 25x_3 + 5x_4 \geq 200 \quad \text{(Carbs)} \\
& 8x_1 + 4x_2 + 0.5x_3 + 0.3x_4 \leq 80 \quad \text{(Fat)} \\
& x_i \geq 0 \quad \forall i
\end{align*}
$$

## PuLP Implementation

```python
from pulp import *

prob = LpProblem("Diet_Problem", LpMinimize)

steak    = LpVariable("Steak",    lowBound=0)
chicken  = LpVariable("Chicken",  lowBound=0)
rice     = LpVariable("Rice",     lowBound=0)
broccoli = LpVariable("Broccoli", lowBound=0)

prob += 2.5*steak + 1.8*chicken + 0.5*rice + 0.6*broccoli

prob += 5*steak + 6*chicken + rice + 2*broccoli >= 50, "Protein"
prob += 2*steak + chicken + 25*rice + 5*broccoli >= 200, "Carbs"
prob += 8*steak + 4*chicken + 0.5*rice + 0.3*broccoli <= 80, "Fat"

prob.solve(PULP_CBC_CMD(msg=0))

print("Status:", LpStatus[prob.status])
print("Total Cost: $", round(value(prob.objective), 2))

for v in prob.variables():
    print(v.name, "=", round(value(v), 2))
